# atlas agent documentation index

This file is the expanded `llms.txt` companion for agents that want more context
without crawling every repository file. Prefer the linked source documents when
exact details matter.

## What atlas is

atlas is a standalone Rust CLI that turns a repository into a compact structural
map for AI coding agents. The map includes signatures, types, imports, reverse
dependencies, file ranks, and optional JSON/XML structure. It excludes function
bodies so the output stays small enough to fit in an agent context window.

Core pipeline:

```text
discover -> parse -> link -> rank -> budget -> render
```

Supported languages today: Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Rust, Go, Java, C,
and C++.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): installation, quickstart, examples, troubleshooting.
- [Docs index](docs/README.md): every guide, grouped by task.
- [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md): local build/test commands and contribution
  expectations.
- [CLAUDE](CLAUDE.md): maintainer workflow rules for agents working in this
  repository.
- [STATUS](STATUS.md): current milestone notes and completed work.
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): public release notes and unreleased changes.

## Using atlas with an agent

- [`atlas-orient` skill](skills/README.md): a drop-in skill that tells an agent to
  orient with a map before exploring; ready-to-paste block in
  [examples/AGENTS.md](examples/AGENTS.md).
- [Agent integration cookbook](docs/agent-cookbook.md): per-agent stdout/file
  recipes (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, terminal agents) and flag guidance.
- [Generate CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md sections](docs/agent-files.md): keep a delimited
  map block fresh without touching manual guidance.
- [Editor task snippets](docs/editor-snippets.md): VS Code / JetBrains / shell.
- [Claude Code MCP setup](docs/CLAUDE_CODE_MCP.md) and
  [examples/claude-code.mcp.json](examples/claude-code.mcp.json).
- [Prompt-injection threat model](docs/prompt-injection.md): maps are untrusted
  data; Markdown vs XML and safe wrappers.

## Reference & tuning

- [Language support matrix](docs/languages.md): extracted symbol kinds,
  visibility rules, import-linking behavior, and caveats per language.
- [`.atlasignore` & monorepo tuning](docs/monorepos.md): ignore syntax and
  focusing large/polyglot repos.
- [Exit codes & error taxonomy](docs/exit-codes.md): the stable `0/1/2` contract.
- [FAQ](docs/FAQ.md), [Windows guide](docs/windows.md),
  [offline/proxy install](docs/install-offline.md),
  [repomap → atlas migration](docs/MIGRATION.md).
- Keep a committed map fresh: [pre-commit hook](docs/pre-commit.md),
  [CI recipes](docs/ci-recipes.md), [API-change gate](docs/ci-diff-gate.md).

## Product and architecture

- [PRD](docs/PRD.md): target users, non-goals, functional requirements,
  success metrics, risks.
- [ADR 0002](docs/adr/0002-index-based-graph.md): index-based graph model.
- [ADR 0003](docs/adr/0003-budget-packing-ladder.md): budget degradation
  ladder.
- [ADR 0004](docs/adr/0004-symbol-index-for-collapsed-tail.md): collapsed-tail
  symbol index.
- [ADR 0005](docs/adr/0005-structural-diff-between-trees.md): structural diff
  design.
- [ADR 0008](docs/adr/0008-mcp-server-over-stdio.md): MCP server over stdio.
- [ADR 0009](docs/adr/0009-stable-symbol-anchors-progressive-disclosure.md):
  stable symbol anchors for progressive disclosure.

## Agent workflows

Generate a Markdown map:

```sh
atlas . --budget 2048
```

Save a map with an agent preamble:

```sh
atlas . --for-agent -o atlas-map.md
```

Focus the map on the area being changed:

```sh
atlas . --focus src/api --focus tests
```

Use structured output:

```sh
atlas . --format json
atlas . --format xml
```

Gate a committed map's freshness (exit 1 if stale — CI / pre-commit):

```sh
atlas . --check ATLAS.md
```

Review structural change between revisions:

```sh
atlas diff HEAD~1 HEAD
atlas diff main . --no-private --exit-code
```

Run the MCP server for compatible clients:

```sh
atlas serve --mcp --root .
```

See [Claude Code MCP setup](docs/CLAUDE_CODE_MCP.md) and
[examples/claude-code.mcp.json](examples/claude-code.mcp.json).

## Benchmarks and claim hygiene

The safest public benchmark claim is from the comprehension benchmark in
[benchmark/history](benchmark/history.md): the default map achieved 20/20
accuracy in both arms while reducing median tokens from 85,670 to 29,781
(-65.2%) and median turns from 3 to 1 on the constrained read-only task suite.

Do not claim a broad edit-task token reduction. The edit-task benchmark is
valuable for characterizing behavior, but the recorded runs are too noisy for a
headline token claim. If you mention edit tasks, say that atlas tends to reduce
turns and helps "find the existing thing" tasks, while token deltas remain
inconclusive until the suite is larger and lower variance.

## Positioning

atlas is not a full-repo concatenator, semantic code search engine, LSP, editor,
or code generator. It is a compact structural index that makes an agent's first
navigation pass cheaper and more grounded.

Adjacent-tool framing:

- Versus full source dumps: atlas preserves navigation signal under a fixed
  token budget and omits implementation bodies.
- Versus ctags/tree-sitter raw output: atlas ranks and budgets the structure for
  LLM consumption.
- Versus Aider repo-map: atlas is standalone, Rust-native, CLI/MCP-oriented, and
  usable with any agent.
- Versus prompt packers: atlas focuses on structural signal, not collecting
  arbitrary file contents.

See [docs/comparison.md](docs/comparison.md).

## Safety and operations

- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): disclosure path and supported versions.
- [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): contributor conduct expectations.
- [docs/PRIVACY.md](docs/PRIVACY.md): local/offline operation and map
  sensitivity.
- [docs/ADOPTION_METRICS.md](docs/ADOPTION_METRICS.md): public-source launch
  metrics without product telemetry.
- [docs/LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md](docs/LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md): release, metadata, and
  community-profile checks.
- [docs/release-readiness.md](docs/release-readiness.md): alpha, beta, and
  stable release-channel gates.
- [docs/post-launch-outreach.md](docs/post-launch-outreach.md): channel-specific
  launch copy and public-source follow-up metrics.

atlas is local-first and read-only in normal use. The MCP server also uses
read-only parsing and rejects paths outside its configured `--root`.
